_metadata.description: Aligned continuity characters — TF Prime (2010-2013), WFC/FOC games (2010-2012), Rescue Bots (2012-2016), RiD 2015 (2015-2017)

characters.name: Optimus Prime
characters.notes: Autobot leader. Voiced by Peter Cullen. Formerly Orion Pax, chosen as Prime by the High Council. Loses memory in S1 finale, mortally wounded in S2 finale, resurrected with new body in S3. Sacrifices himself to restore the AllSpark in Predacons Rising. Also playable in WFC and FOC games

characters.name: Bumblebee
characters.notes: Autobot scout. Cannot speak for most of the series due to damaged voice box (courtesy of Megatron). Partnered with Raf. Leads Team Prime forces in the final battle. Also playable in WFC and FOC games

characters.name: Arcee
characters.notes: Autobot warrior. Partnered with Jack Darby. Lost previous partners Tailgate and Cliffjumper. Fierce fighter with a personal vendetta against Airachnid. Voiced by Sumalee Montano. Also appears in WFC game

characters.name: Bulkhead
characters.notes: Autobot heavy hitter and former Wrecker. Partnered with Miko. Despite his size, has a gentle personality. Severely injured in S2 by Hardshell. Voiced by Kevin Michael Richardson. Also appears in WFC game

characters.name: Ratchet
characters.notes: Autobot medic and scientist. Team Prime's doctor and tech specialist. Grumpy but deeply caring. Briefly experiments with synthetic energon. Voiced by Jeffrey Combs. Also playable in WFC game

characters.name: Cliffjumper
characters.notes: Autobot warrior. Killed by Starscream in the pilot episode, establishing the series' willingness to kill characters. Voiced by Dwayne Johnson. Appears in flashbacks. Also playable in FOC

characters.name: Wheeljack
characters.alt_mode: White sports car with green and red stripes
characters.notes: Autobot Wrecker and commando. Former partner of Bulkhead. A lone wolf who gradually integrates with Team Prime. Expert swordsman and demolitions specialist. Voiced by James Horan. Series: Prime

characters.name: Smokescreen
characters.alt_mode: Blue and yellow sports car with racing number 38
characters.notes: Autobot Elite Guard rookie who joins Team Prime in S2. Trained by Alpha Trion. Nearly chosen as a new Prime. Uses the Phase Shifter relic. Restores Optimus with the Forge of Solus Prime. Voiced by Nolan North. Series: Prime

characters.name: Ultra Magnus
characters.alt_mode: Blue armored truck with missile launchers
characters.notes: Autobot commander and second-in-command. Arrives on Earth in S3 to reinforce Team Prime after the Autobot base is destroyed. Strict, by-the-book leader who wields the Forge of Solus Prime as a hammer weapon. Voiced by Michael Ironside. Series: Prime

characters.name: Megatron
characters.notes: Decepticon leader. Discovers and uses Dark Energon. Nearly killed by Bumblebee in S1, body possessed by Unicron in Predacons Rising. Voluntarily disbands the Decepticons and exiles himself after Unicron's defeat. Voiced by Frank Welker. Also playable in WFC and FOC games

characters.name: Starscream
characters.notes: Decepticon Air Commander and perpetual schemer. Takes command when Megatron is injured. Repeatedly attempts and fails to seize leadership. Goes rogue in S1 before returning. Voiced by Steve Blum. Also playable in WFC and FOC games

characters.name: Soundwave
characters.notes: Decepticon communications chief. Silent and menacing — rarely speaks, replays recordings instead. Deploys Laserbeak as a spy. Utterly loyal to Megatron. Banished to the Shadowzone in the finale. Voiced by Frank Welker. Also playable in WFC and FOC games

characters.name: Knock Out
characters.notes: Decepticon medic. Vain and obsessed with his finish. Partner of Breakdown. Performs experiments on captives. Defects to the Autobots in the finale, claiming he's 'joining the winning team.' Voiced by Daran Norris. Series: Prime

characters.name: Breakdown
characters.notes: Decepticon bruiser and Knock Out's partner. Rival of Bulkhead. Captured by MECH, who harvest his body. Later killed by Airachnid. His body is used by Silas as a cyborg shell. Also appears in WFC game. Series: Prime

characters.name: Dreadwing
characters.notes: Decepticon warrior and Skyquake's twin brother. Arrives on Earth in S2 seeking vengeance for his brother. Honorable and loyal to Megatron until learning Starscream desecrated Skyquake's corpse. Killed by Megatron for attempting to kill Starscream. Voiced by Tony Todd. Series: Prime

characters.name: Skyquake
characters.notes: Decepticon warrior and Dreadwing's twin brother. Loyally guarded a post on Earth for millennia. Killed in S1 episode 'Masters and Students.' His corpse is later reanimated as a Terrorcon by Starscream using Dark Energon. Series: Prime

characters.name: Airachnid
characters.notes: Decepticon rogue and sadistic hunter. Spider-like robot mode. Killed Arcee's former partner Tailgate. Goes rogue from the Decepticons and takes control of an Insecticon hive. Trapped in stasis on a moon. Voiced by Gina Torres. Series: Prime

characters.name: Shockwave
characters.notes: Decepticon scientist. Single optic, logical and ruthless. Guarded Cybertron while Megatron was on Earth. Clones the Predacons in S3 from fossilized remains. His experiments create Predaking, Darksteel, and Skylynx. Also appears in WFC and FOC games. Series: Prime

characters.name: Predaking
characters.notes: Predacon leader. Cloned by Shockwave from ancient CNA. Unexpectedly develops intelligence and transforms. Megatron orders the other Predacon clones destroyed when he realizes Predaking's threat. Allies with Autobots against Unicron in Predacons Rising. Series: Prime

characters.name: Darksteel
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. One of two additional Predacons cloned by Shockwave alongside Skylynx. Appears in Predacons Rising TV movie. Less intelligent than Predaking. Allies with the Autobots against Unicron's Terrorcon army. Series: Prime (Predacons Rising)

characters.name: Skylynx
characters.notes: Predacon warrior. One of two additional Predacons cloned by Shockwave alongside Darksteel. Appears in Predacons Rising TV movie. Less intelligent than Predaking. Allies with the Autobots against Unicron's Terrorcon army. Not to be confused with G1 Sky Lynx. Series: Prime (Predacons Rising)

characters.name: Hardshell
characters.notes: Insecticon commander and the only individually named Insecticon in the Prime series. Severely injures Bulkhead. Later killed by Miko using the Apex Armor. The Insecticons in Prime are a hive-minded swarm of warriors loyal to the strongest leader. Series: Prime

characters.name: Makeshift
characters.notes: Decepticon spy with shape-shifting abilities. Infiltrates the Autobot base by impersonating Wheeljack in S1. Killed when exposed and ejected through a GroundBridge. Series: Prime

characters.name: Laserbeak
characters.notes: Soundwave's Mini-Con deployer. Used primarily as a spy and scout, attaching to Soundwave's chest when not deployed. Does not speak. Series: Prime

characters.name: Jack Darby
characters.notes: Human teenager partnered with Arcee. Acts as the de facto leader of the three human kids. Uses the Key to Vector Sigma to restore Optimus Prime's memory. Son of June Darby. Series: Prime

characters.name: Miko Nakadai
characters.notes: Japanese exchange student partnered with Bulkhead. Impulsive and thrill-seeking, constantly sneaks into danger. Uses the Apex Armor in battle against Hardshell. Series: Prime

characters.name: Rafael Esquivel
characters.notes: Young human genius partnered with Bumblebee. Youngest of the three kids but the most technically skilled. Can understand Bumblebee's beeps. Interfaces with the Key to Vector Sigma. Called 'Raf.' Series: Prime

characters.name: Agent Fowler
characters.notes: Special Agent William Fowler, US government liaison to the Autobots. Former Army Ranger. Works for Unit:E. Provides military support and political cover for Team Prime. Voiced by Ernie Hudson. Series: Prime

characters.name: June Darby
characters.notes: Jack Darby's mother, a nurse. Initially unaware of the Transformers. Becomes involved when her son's secret is revealed. Develops a rapport with Agent Fowler. Series: Prime

characters.name: Silas
characters.notes: Colonel Leland Bishop, leader of MECH — a human terrorist organization that seeks Cybertronian technology. Mortally wounded and grafted into Breakdown's corpse as a cyborg. Eventually captured and experimented on by Knock Out. Series: Prime

characters.name: Unicron
characters.alt_mode: Earth itself (his body forms the planet's core)
characters.notes: The Chaos Bringer, brother of Primus. His body forms the core of Earth. His consciousness awakens when Cybertron begins to be restored. Possesses Megatron's body in Predacons Rising. Sealed away by Optimus Prime using the AllSpark. Series: Prime

characters.name: Primus
characters.notes: The creator god of the Transformers. His body IS the planet Cybertron. Brother and eternal enemy of Unicron. Referenced throughout the series but never directly appears. Creator of the Thirteen original Primes. Series: Prime

characters.name: Alpha Trion
characters.notes: Ancient Autobot and one of the Thirteen original Primes. Mentor who placed Iacon relics on Earth. Trained Smokescreen. Appears in visions and the Covenant of Primus. Voiced by George Takei. Series: Prime

characters.name: Tailgate
characters.notes: Autobot warrior and Arcee's former partner before Cliffjumper. Killed by Airachnid on Cybertron. Appears only in flashbacks. His death deeply affected Arcee's personality. Series: Prime

characters.name: Sentinel Zeta Prime
characters.notes: Autobot leader who preceded Optimus Prime. Killed by Megatron in War for Cybertron after Soundwave drained his spark via the Omega Key. His death led to Optimus being chosen as the new Prime. Also known as Zeta Prime (game) or Sentinel Prime (novels). Series: WFC

characters.name: Ironhide
characters.notes: Autobot weapons specialist. Playable campaign character in WFC. Tough veteran warrior. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Sideswipe
characters.notes: Autobot warrior. Playable campaign character in WFC. Fast melee combatant. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC. Also appears in RiD 2015 with a new design

characters.name: Jetfire
characters.notes: Autobot aerial warrior and scientist. Playable campaign character in WFC. Leads the Aerialbot missions on Cybertron. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Air Raid
characters.notes: Autobot Aerialbot. Playable campaign character in WFC alongside Jetfire and Silverbolt. Daring and reckless flyer. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Silverbolt
characters.notes: Autobot Aerialbot leader. Playable campaign character in WFC alongside Jetfire and Air Raid. Brave despite a secret fear of heights. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Warpath
characters.notes: Autobot tank. Playable campaign character in WFC. Loud, boisterous warrior who loves combat. Known for his verbal sound effects (BLAM! POW!). Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Jazz
characters.notes: Autobot special operations agent. Playable in FOC campaign and WFC DLC. Cool and versatile. Uses a grappling hook. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC/FOC. Also appears in RiD 2015

characters.name: Omega Supreme
characters.notes: Massive Autobot guardian. Boss fight in WFC Decepticon campaign — the last line of defense for the Omega Key. Damaged by Megatron's Dark Energon corruption. One of the largest Autobots. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Metroplex
characters.notes: Gigantic Autobot city-former integrated into Iacon's structure. Awakens in FOC when Optimus pulls a lever at the city's core. Deals a near-lethal blow to Megatron. Sacrifices his remaining energy to launch the Ark. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC

characters.name: Grimlock
characters.notes: Dinobot leader. Playable in FOC campaign. Captured and experimented on by Shockwave, giving him his dinosaur alt mode. Extremely powerful but rage-driven. Transforms by building up rage energy. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC. Also appears in RiD 2015 as a main character with a new dinosaur design

characters.name: Swoop
characters.notes: Dinobot aerial warrior. Appears in FOC campaign alongside Grimlock. One of the Lightning Strike Coalition members experimented on by Shockwave. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC

characters.name: Slug
characters.notes: Dinobot warrior (called Slag in G1). Appears in FOC campaign. One of the Lightning Strike Coalition members experimented on by Shockwave. Hot-headed fighter. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC

characters.name: Snarl
characters.notes: Dinobot warrior. Appears in FOC campaign. One of the Lightning Strike Coalition members experimented on by Shockwave. Quiet and solitary. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC

characters.name: Barricade
characters.notes: Decepticon scout. Playable campaign character in WFC alongside Megatron and Brawl. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Brawl
characters.notes: Combaticon heavy weapons specialist. Playable in WFC campaign alongside Megatron. Also a component of Bruticus in FOC. Forms the left leg of Bruticus. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC/FOC

characters.name: Thundercracker
characters.notes: Decepticon Seeker. Playable in WFC campaign alongside Starscream and Skywarp. Has doubts about the Decepticon cause but follows orders. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Skywarp
characters.notes: Decepticon Seeker with teleportation abilities. Playable in WFC campaign alongside Starscream and Thundercracker. Not particularly bright but dangerous. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Trypticon
characters.notes: Massive Decepticon weapon. Boss fight in WFC Autobot campaign. Crashes to Earth and becomes the Decepticon warship Nemesis. Does not appear in the Prime TV show directly, though the Nemesis features prominently. Series: WFC

characters.name: Onslaught
characters.notes: Combaticon commander and torso of Bruticus. Strategic mastermind. WFC DLC character, appears in FOC. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC/FOC

characters.name: Blast Off
characters.notes: Combaticon space warrior and right arm of Bruticus. Aloof and considers himself superior. Appears in FOC. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC

characters.name: Vortex
characters.notes: Combaticon interrogator and left arm of Bruticus. Playable in FOC campaign. Sadistic and enjoys aerial combat. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC

characters.name: Swindle
characters.notes: Combaticon arms dealer and right leg of Bruticus. Playable in FOC campaign. Opportunistic and self-serving. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC

characters.name: Bruticus
characters.notes: Combaticon combined form. Formed from Onslaught (torso), Blast Off (right arm), Vortex (left arm), Swindle (right leg), and Brawl (left leg). Playable as a boss-level character in FOC. Incredibly powerful but unintelligent. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: FOC

characters.name: Slipstream
characters.notes: Female Decepticon Seeker. Appears in WFC as a playable character. Named after the Animated continuity character. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Scattershot
characters.notes: Autobot warrior. WFC DLC character. Aggressive and trigger-happy. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Dead End
characters.notes: Decepticon warrior. WFC DLC character. Fatalistic and nihilistic. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Demolishor
characters.notes: Decepticon heavy. WFC DLC character. Powerful but slow. Does not appear in the Prime TV show. Series: WFC

characters.name: Heatwave
characters.notes: Leader of Rescue Force Sigma-17. Appointed team leader by Optimus Prime on Griffin Rock. Later gains a fireboat mode. Voiced by Steve Blum. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Chase
characters.notes: Rescue Bot who transforms into a police cruiser. By-the-book and rule-following. Partners with Chief Charlie Burns. Voiced by D.C. Douglas. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Blades
characters.notes: Rescue Bot who transforms into a helicopter despite being afraid of heights. Partners with Dani Burns. Later gains a pterodactyl mode. Voiced by Parvesh Cheena. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Boulder
characters.notes: Rescue Bot who transforms into a construction vehicle. Artistic and intellectual, fascinated by Earth culture. Partners with Graham Burns. Voiced by Imari Williams. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Salvage
characters.notes: Rescue Bot who finds value in discarded materials. Joins the team later in the series. Voiced by Jason Marsden. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Blurr
characters.notes: Fast-talking, speed-obsessed Rescue Bot. Joins the team in later seasons. Voiced by Max Mittelman. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: High Tide
characters.notes: Old-school Autobot who transforms into a rescue ship. Can combine with his ship for a Megabot Mode. Also has a submarine mode. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Quickshadow
characters.notes: British-accented female Rescue Bot who transforms into a luxury sports car with right-hand drive. Voiced by Alex Kingston. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Hot Shot
characters.notes: Rescue Bots Academy recruit. Enthusiastic and eager to prove himself. Later gains additional jet, hovercraft, and fire truck modes via Mul-T-Cog. Voiced by Pierce Cravens. Series: Rescue Bots Academy

characters.name: Whirl
characters.notes: Rescue Bots Academy recruit. Aspires to be a police officer like Chase. Later acquires a police car alt mode. Voiced by Courtney Shaw. Series: Rescue Bots Academy

characters.name: Hoist
characters.notes: Rescue Bots Academy recruit. Aspiring inventor and mechanic. Transforms into a teal green tow truck. Voiced by Alan Trinca. Series: Rescue Bots Academy

characters.name: Medix
characters.notes: Rescue Bots Academy recruit. Medical-focused bot. Voiced by Adam Andrianopoulous. Series: Rescue Bots Academy

characters.name: Wedge
characters.notes: Rescue Bots Academy recruit. Construction-focused bot who later scans a sports car for speed. Voiced by Mason Hensley. Series: Rescue Bots Academy

characters.name: Chief Charlie Burns
characters.notes: Chief of police and fire on Griffin Rock. Father of four children. Partners with Chase. Voiced by Maurice LaMarche. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Kade Burns
characters.notes: Eldest Burns sibling. Firefighter who partners with Heatwave. Brash and overconfident. Voiced by Jason Marsden. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Dani Burns
characters.notes: Burns daughter. Helicopter pilot who partners with Blades. Voiced by Lacey Chabert. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Graham Burns
characters.notes: Burns son. Engineer who partners with Boulder. Voiced by Shannon McKain. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Cody Burns
characters.notes: Youngest Burns child. Acts as liaison between the Rescue Bots and humans. Not officially partnered with a specific bot but closest to all of them. Voiced by Elan Garfias (RB), Andy Zou (RBA). Series: Rescue Bots / Rescue Bots Academy

characters.name: Doc Greene
characters.notes: Griffin Rock's eccentric scientist. Full name Ezra Greene. His inventions frequently endanger the island. Father of Frankie Greene. Voiced by LeVar Burton. Series: Rescue Bots

characters.name: Strongarm
characters.notes: By-the-book Autobot cadet. Law enforcement officer who follows regulations strictly. Component of Ultra Bee combiner (arm). Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Fixit
characters.notes: Autobot Mini-Con who served as caretaker of the Alchemor prison ship. Team technician and support specialist. Prone to verbal glitches. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Drift
characters.notes: Samurai-themed Autobot bounty hunter. Master of Jetstorm and Slipstream. Functions as a sword for Ultra Bee combiner. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Jetstorm
characters.notes: Autobot Mini-Con student of Drift. Trains alongside Slipstream. Can deploy from Drift in combat. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Slipstream (Mini-Con)
characters.notes: Autobot Mini-Con student of Drift. Trains alongside Jetstorm. Can deploy from Drift in combat. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Windblade
characters.notes: Female Autobot who can create powerful wind blasts. Transforms into a VTOL jet. First animated appearance of the fan-created character. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Denny Clay
characters.notes: Owner of a scrapyard that becomes the Autobots' base of operations. Father of Russell. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Russell Clay
characters.notes: Son of Denny Clay. Befriends the Autobots and helps with missions. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Steeljaw
characters.notes: Primary Decepticon antagonist. Wolf-like robot who seeks to build a Decepticon army on Earth. Cunning and charismatic leader. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Underbite
characters.notes: Chompazoid Decepticon. Grows stronger by consuming metal. One of the first escaped prisoners from the Alchemor. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Thunderhoof
characters.notes: Moose-like Decepticon crime boss. Speaks with an exaggerated accent. Escaped Alchemor prisoner. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Fracture
characters.notes: Decepticon bounty hunter. Rival of Drift. Accompanied by Mini-Con partners. Transforms into a chopper motorcycle. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Clampdown
characters.notes: Crustacean-like Decepticon. Cowardly and paranoid. Transforms into a wood-paneled hatchback. Escaped Alchemor prisoner. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Overload
characters.notes: Powerful Decepticon who transforms into a Cybertronian tank. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Glowstrike
characters.notes: Cold and aloof Decepticon leader. Insect-like with ability to fire energy bolts from her palms. Commands Decepticon forces from a secret base. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Scorponok
characters.notes: Decepticon who transforms into a scorpion. Large and powerful with articulated claws and stinger. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Saberhorn
characters.notes: Gentleman swordsman Decepticon. Transforms into a winged rhinoceros beetle. Skilled dual-wielding swordfighter. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Ultra Bee
characters.notes: Combined form of Bumblebee (torso), Sideswipe (arm), Strongarm (arm), and Grimlock (legs). Drift functions as a sword. Has no unified personality — components must agree on actions. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Menasor
characters.notes: Combined form of the Stunticons: Motormaster (torso), Heatseeker (left leg), Wildbreak (left arm), Dragstrip (right arm), and Slashmark (right leg). Clashing component personalities hamper coordination. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Motormaster
characters.notes: Stunticon leader. Forms the torso of Menasor. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Heatseeker
characters.notes: Stunticon who forms the left leg of Menasor. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Wildbreak
characters.notes: Stunticon who forms the left arm of Menasor. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Dragstrip
characters.notes: Stunticon who forms the right arm of Menasor. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Slashmark
characters.notes: Stunticon who forms the right leg of Menasor. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Galvatronus
characters.notes: Combined form of the corrupt Autobot High Council members: Cyclonus (torso), Cyberwarp, Skyjack, Treadshock, and Riotgear. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Cyclonus
characters.notes: Corrupt Autobot High Council member who is secretly a Decepticon. Forms the torso of Galvatronus. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Cyberwarp
characters.notes: Corrupt council member who combines as part of Galvatronus. Space-fighter scout who forms an arm. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Skyjack
characters.notes: Corrupt council member who combines as part of Galvatronus. Space-fighter scout who forms an arm. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Treadshock
characters.notes: Corrupt council member who combines as part of Galvatronus. Ground-vehicle scout who forms a leg. Series: RiD 2015

characters.name: Riotgear
characters.notes: Corrupt council member who combines as part of Galvatronus. Ground-vehicle scout who forms a leg. Series: RiD 2015
